case.title: BERTERA'S HOPEWELL FOODLAND, INC. v. MASTERS

opinion.h4: BERTERA'S HOPEWELL FOODLAND, INC. v. MASTERS, (1968)
BERTERA'S HOPEWELL FOODLAND, INC. v. MASTERS, DISTRICT ATTORNEY, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 1132. Decided April 8, 1968.
opinion.p: Hubert I. Teitelbaum and Martin M. Sheinman for appellant.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: INC. VILLAGE, P. JEFFERSON v. BD. OF SUPERS., SUFFOLK, (1968)
opinion.p: INCORPORATED VILLAGE OF PORT JEFFERSON ET AL. v. BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE COUNTY OF SUFFOLK ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 1152. Decided April 8, 1968.
Stanley S. Corwin for Board of Supervisors of Suffolk County et al., and Frederick Mars for Receiver of Taxes of the Town of Brookhaven, appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
